figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of factor models (k = 3, 20 S&P 500 stocks, 2015–2024)

Figure 1: KAN nonlinear factors (2015–2024). The red dashed line marks the COVID-19 crash of March 2020. All three factors exhibit sharp spikes at this date, demonstrating that the model treats the crash as qualitatively distinct from normal market movements.

Figure 2: Learned edge functions φ(x) for all 20 stocks (contribution to Factor 1). If relationships were linear as PCA assumes, all curves would be straight lines. They are not, providing direct visual evidence of nonlinear factor structure.
